basics.name: David (Jiachen) Weng
basics.label: Content Producer & Visual Designer
basics.summary: Content producer, visual designer, and creative technologist in Vancouver. I build things that look good and work well. From brand identities to open-source apps, my work spans graphic design, video production, event management, and AI-integrated workflows. I maintain open-source projects like OpenKara (desktop karaoke app, Tauri + Rust). Co-Founder of OwO Network, LLC.

work.name: BC + AI Ecosystem
work.summary: Lead visual content operations for a growing AI community in British Columbia.
highlights: Designed 50+ branded assets for social media, events, and presentations reaching 3,000+ cumulative viewers
Managed multi-platform content publishing workflow across LinkedIn, Instagram, and YouTube, maintaining brand consistency across 3 channels
Established brand guidelines and asset library, standardizing visual identity across 5+ organizational partners

work.name: OwO Network, LLC
work.summary: Building tools that make AI accessible for everyday creative workflows.
highlights: Co-founded technology company, defining product roadmap and coordinating cross-functional development
Led product strategy and technical architecture decisions for AI-integrated consumer applications

work.name: UBC Student Housing and Hospitality Services
work.summary: Planned and delivered community programs for a residence community of 1,500+ students.
highlights: Designed and executed 12+ residential programs per academic year, engaging 25–60+ attendees per event
Managed community of 1,500+ residents through structured unit visits, peer mentoring, and wellness check-ins
Facilitated cross-cultural programming promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion among diverse resident populations

work.name: Citang (词堂)
work.summary: Create, contextualize, and curate internet culture content with cultural commentary for a bilingual audience.
highlights: Created and contextualized internet culture content, driving 150,090 views on a single post and contributing to 5,000+ follower growth over 6 months

volunteer.summary: Managed all financial activities for the Media Studies Student Association.
highlights: Managed association budget and financial operations over 2-year term, maintaining accurate records and on-time reporting

volunteer.organization: UBC Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA)
volunteer.summary: Directed all media production for UBC's Chinese Students and Scholars Association, serving 1,000+ community members.
highlights: Built and led media team of 8+ volunteers, producing 100+ event photos and 5+ edited videos per term
Created visual content strategy that drove measurable attendance growth across flagship cultural events

awards.title: Faculty of Arts International Student Scholarship

publications.name: Transcending Barriers: Viewing Practices of 'Games of Thrones' in China, India, and Russia
publications.publisher: Beacon: UBC Journal of Media Studies
publications.summary: Cross-cultural media consumption research examining unauthorized viewing practices across three countries.

projects.name: OpenKara
projects.description: Open-source desktop karaoke application using AI vocal separation and online synced lyrics.
highlights: Built with Tauri 2 + Rust, demonstrating full-stack development and product design
